queries.query: audit our AGENTS.md — is it hand-curated, are the CLAUDE.md and cursor mirrors in sync, and do the rules trace to real failures?
curate an llms.txt for our docs so an agent fetches the right pages first instead of crawling the whole site
our coding agent keeps calling the wrong tool because two MCP tool descriptions are nearly identical — fix the descriptions
will our reference docs survive retrieval? agents pull a paragraph that says 'see above' and can't use it
our always-loaded context file is huge — what should the agent load up front vs retrieve on demand?
design the AGENTS.md contract for our repo so agents know our commands, invariants, and load-bearing rules
full agent-docs review across our AGENTS.md, llms.txt, tool descriptions, reference, and context budget — score each
our skill exists but the agent never triggers it — tune the description so it activates on the right prompts
audit our human docs site and help center for confusing onboarding and zero-result search
design the typed tool schema and structured-output validation for our Agent SDK
scaffold our repo's AGENTS.md, hooks, and CI gates and wire the do-not-autogenerate enforcement
set up a trace-and-eval loop and watch our agent's quality drift in production
promote this recurring agent failure into an AGENTS.md rule from our reflection log
tighten the prose in our marketing landing page

review our llms.txt and machine-readable reference for whether an AI agent can actually consume them — stable anchors, retrieval-safe chunks, no 'see above' — this is our agent-native layer, not the human docs site
review our AGENTS.md — are the invariants phrased and placed so an agent actually follows them, instead of getting ignored?
